steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says participants perceived a broken line as continuous even though the break was in a blocked area. The blank completes: "the brain can ______, filling in what is missing." The word must mean the brain makes up for or supplies missing visual information.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘captivate’: To captivate means to charm or fascinate an audience. The brain does not captivate missing information — this word describes an effect on people, not a cognitive process. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘revert’: To revert means to return to a previous state. The brain reverting filling in information is incoherent — there is no prior state being returned to. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘barricade’: To barricade means to block or obstruct. This directly contradicts the finding: the brain completed the broken line rather than blocking perception of it. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘compensate’: To compensate means to make up for something lacking. "The brain can compensate, filling in what is missing" perfectly matches the study's result. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
